statement: A uniform solid disk of mass 6 kg and radius 0.4 m rolls without slipping down a 20° incline. Find the linear acceleration of the center of the disk and the friction force. I_G = ½·m·r². Use g = 9.81 m/s².
common_student_errors: Assuming the disk slides (ignoring the rolling constraint a = α·r), which overestimates the acceleration
Neglecting that friction acts up the slope (not down) for rolling without slipping
Using I_G = m·r² instead of ½·m·r² for a solid disk
